lines.speaker: Doris
lines.text: Hello again dearie. How are you doing?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pretty good!

lines.speaker: Doris
lines.text: That's good to hear.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not too good actually!

lines.speaker: Doris
lines.text: Oh well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's it like living so close to the wilderness?

lines.speaker: Doris
lines.text: Oh, it's not all that bad. It was a bit scary at first but as long as I don't go past the warning signs I'm all right.

lines.speaker: Doris
lines.text: It's actually pretty quiet here. I used to live in Lumbridge and, let me tell you, that's the dangerous place for people like me.

lines.speaker: Doris
lines.text: Thieves were picking people's pockets with impunity, and killing them on the streets in broad daylight!

lines.speaker: Doris
lines.text: And there weren't even any guards to protect us! Not that the guards in the other cities do much good to protect people from what I've heard.

lines.speaker: Doris
lines.text: I just thank goodness I haven't got an Attack option any more! I wouldn't last five minutes!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How did Dave come to be evil?

lines.speaker: Doris
lines.text: Oh, I don't know really. He's always been evil, I think. Maybe growing up so close to the wilderness has affected him in some way.

lines.speaker: Doris
lines.text: When he was little he used to want to go into Edgeville dungeon. You know how kids are, always wanting to be dungeon adventurers! But he didn't want to be a hero, he wanted to be a monster!

lines.speaker: Doris
lines.text: And then he spent all summer building a tree-house. Except it wasn't a tree-house, of course, it was his Tower of Fear!

lines.speaker: Doris
lines.text: Of course I was hoping he'd have grown out of it by now and maybe even left home. But at least it keeps him happy.

sections.title: While having a hellcat as a follower

lines.speaker: Doris
lines.text: Hello again dearie. How are you doing?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's happened to my cat?

lines.speaker: Doris
lines.text: Oh, poor thing. The hell-rats do that, you know. I think a nice drink of milk would sort it out. Shall I give it one?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please!

lines.speaker: Doris
lines.text: Here puss...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I like it this way.

lines.speaker: Doris
lines.text: Well, I suppose as long as the cat is all right, it doesn't matter what it looks like.
